name: Matter of Time
tips: 4-mana single-target removal + burn DoT + Dark Matter ramp — one of the most efficient tempo plays in the Dark toolbox once your pool hits 4+.
Damage equals your Dark Matter POOL (a player resource, visible to both sides). Bank pool before casting for maximum burst.
The burn is the clutch — a partial-kill target at low 🤍 dies in its owner's next Decay Phase from the burn tick. Use Matter of Time to 'finish later' when you can't quite one-shot.
Pair with [[Flame Wyrm]] — its Passive adds +1 to burn tick damage, turning Matter of Time's burn into a heavier long-tail threat.
Redundant against already-burning targets: the burn effect is a boolean, so casting a second Matter of Time on the same minion wastes the burn half. Chain onto a fresh target instead.
rulings: Damage equals your Dark Matter pool when the cast RESOLVES (after the react window closes). At 0 Dark Matter the damage is 0 (the burn and the gain still resolve).
Both the damage and the [[Burn]] hit the same chosen target — one target pick resolves all three effect lines.
Burn is a boolean [[Burning]] status — re-applying it to an already-burning minion is a no-op; the first burn persists until the minion dies.

The gain credits YOUR pool: +1 Dark Matter per friendly [[Dark Mage]] on the board (element Dark AND the Mage tribe; composite tribes count since 2026-07-10). It applies after damage and burn resolve, so newly-gained Dark Matter does not scale THIS cast.
If the target dies to the damage, burn is applied post-mortem to a dead minion and has no effect (the grave doesn't tick).
trivia: Matter of Time is the 4-mana sibling of [[Dark Matter Barrage]] — same pool scaling, same Dark Mage ramp payoff, narrower target range but cheaper and carries a persistent burn.
